title: Substack vs Moosend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack vs Moosend: pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
intro: Substack and Moosend serve different purposes in the email marketing space. Substack is optimized for individual writers and creators monetizing content directly, while Moosend is built for small businesses and marketers who need comprehensive automation tools. Choosing between them depends on whether you prioritize monetization or advanced marketing features.

comparison_table.tool_b: Freemium with affordable paid plans starting at $9/month

comparison_table.tool_a: Unlimited subscribers, no payment processing fees until you monetize
comparison_table.tool_b: Up to 1,000 subscribers with unlimited emails

comparison_table.tool_a: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, built-in audience discovery
comparison_table.tool_b: Automation workflows, landing pages, A/B testing, real-time analytics

comparison_table.tool_a: Individual writers and creators monetizing newsletters
comparison_table.tool_b: Small businesses running email marketing campaigns

choose_a_if: You're a writer or creator wanting to monetize directly from your audience
You need unlimited subscribers on a free plan with no payment processing fees
You want integrated podcast hosting and content discovery features
choose_b_if: You need advanced automation workflows and marketing campaign tools
You want unlimited emails with affordable paid plans starting at just $9/month
You require landing pages, A/B testing, and detailed analytics for your campaigns

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—they serve different purposes. Substack excels for individual creators monetizing content, while Moosend is superior for businesses needing marketing automation and analytics. Your choice depends on whether monetization or advanced features matter more to your goals.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Moosend?
faq.a: Substack is free indefinitely with no subscriber limits, but takes 10% of paid subscription revenue. Moosend is free up to 1,000 subscribers, then costs from $9/month. For growing businesses, Moosend often provides better value; for monetizing writers, Substack's revenue-share model may be more economical.

faq.q: Can I switch from Moosend to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your subscriber list from Moosend and import it to Substack, though the process requires manual steps. However, switching depends on your goals—if you need marketing automation, staying with Moosend is recommended, as Substack lacks those advanced features.
